impact: At ≤1100px the fit-grid drops the persistent left module browser and exposes a "Browse Modules" toggle (fitting_tool.html:55). The toggle's onclick handler positions the panel `fixed; left:0; top:0; bottom:0; z-index:50; width:300px` — full-height overlay with no backdrop click-to-close, no X button, no ESC handler, no focus trap. Mobile/tablet users who open the browser are stranded — they can scroll the browser pane and tap modules, but there is no obvious way to close it short of refreshing or tapping the toggle a second time (which the toggle's onclick does NOT do — it only opens). Recommended: convert to a real modal pattern reusing the existing info-modal style at line 171 (backdrop, click-outside-to-close, X button), add ESC binding, and toggle aria-hidden on the panel. Extract the inline onclick handler into a function so the close path can be unit-testable.
resolution: Fixed in commit 95698ce. Mobile module-browser overlay now has three dismiss paths: (1) backdrop click (full-screen rgba(0,0,0,0.55) under z-index:49), (2) X close button in panel header (mobile-only via media query), (3) ESC keydown. openModuleBrowser/closeModuleBrowser functions handle data-overlay attribute toggling. Replaced the inline onclick blob on #browser-toggle with data-click="openModuleBrowser" aligned with T-031 delegation. Panel default styles moved from inline style= into CSS so the close path can cleanly wipe inline overrides without breaking the desktop sticky layout. Body scroll-locked while overlay is open.
title: Fitting tool's mobile module-browser overlay has no dismiss affordance
